📚 Quick Reference

Where to find what you need in the app

Weekly scores
Go to My Weekly Development — your full week-by-week table with AHT, CSAT, SQS and more.
CSAT by case
Go to Client Satisfaction — see every case, whether it passed or failed, with full context on the right.
TL feedback
Go to Feedback Tracker — weekly Operational and Quality feedback logged by your Team Leader.
Action plans
Go to My Registered Actions — development tasks your TL has created, with due dates and status.
Quality audits
Go to Support Quality Score Split — each QA audit with Case Handling, Compliance, Communication scores.
Monthly trends
Go to Monthly Development — your performance rolled up month by month.
Team results
Go to Team Performance — your full team's weekly results so you can see where you stand.
Share an idea
Use Share your ideas on the app from the home screen to submit suggestions directly to the team.
Report a bug
Use Report an issue from the home screen — describe the problem and it goes straight to the app team.
My profile
Go to My Profile — check your Site, Workday ID, Market, Team Leader, and Team Manager details.

Training Overview

Welcome to Your GSO Reviewers App Training

This guide walks you through everything you need to know about your new performance app. Work through each section at your own pace, or jump straight to what you need.

The GSO Reviewers App gives you full visibility of your own performance — CSAT, Quality Score, case feedback, development trends, TL feedback, and registered action plans — without chasing anyone or digging through spreadsheets. It updates automatically and is available anytime. This training will show you exactly how to use it.

❓

Overview · 7 sections · ~24 min total
Section 01

What Is This App & Why Does It Exist? ⏱ 1 min

The GSO Reviewers App was built specifically for you — to give you full visibility into your own performance without chasing anyone for data.

Before this app, getting a clear picture of your performance meant waiting for your TL to update a spreadsheet, receiving feedback buried in a Google Chat scroll, and having no single place to see your CSAT, Quality, development scores, TL feedback, or action plans together. That ends now. The GSO Reviewers App puts everything in one place — automatically updated, always accessible, just for you.

💡

Good to know: This app works alongside the GSO Feedback App used by your Team Leader and QA. When your TL logs feedback, creates an action plan, or records a weekly review — it all appears in your app automatically. Both apps are synced in real time.

1 of 7
Section 02

What You Can See & Do ⏱ 3 min

Here's a full breakdown of every feature available to you inside the app.

📈

Weekly Development

Track your performance week by week. Data updates automatically — no manual refresh needed.

📅

Monthly Development

Get a broader view of your growth trends across the full month to spot patterns and progress.

⭐

Support Quality Score

See your current Quality score in-app. No more asking your TL — your score is always current.

😊

Client Satisfaction (CSAT)

Monitor your CSAT score in real time alongside your Quality score — one unified view.

👥

Team Performance

See how your team is performing as a whole. Understand where you stand within the broader landscape.

New
📋

Feedback Tracker

Weekly feedback from your TL recorded and visible in-app. Includes Operational and Quality feedback, plus an option to receive it by email.

New
✅

My Registered Actions

Action plans your TL creates for your development appear here — task, start date, due date, status, and comments.

👤

My Profile

Manage your personal profile information and keep your details up to date.

💡

Share Your Ideas

Have a suggestion? Submit ideas and feedback about the app directly through the platform.

✅

Tip: Feedback Tracker and My Registered Actions are both read-only for you — your TL creates them on their side and they appear here automatically.

2 of 7
Section 03

See It In Action ⏱ 9 min

A screen-by-screen walkthrough of every section in your GSO Reviewers App — so nothing is a surprise when you log in for the first time.

🏠 Your Home Screen

The first thing you see when you log in. Your name, the latest data date, and quick-access buttons to every section of the app.

📈 My Weekly Development

Your week-by-week performance table. AHT, CSAT%, Survey Count, DSat, CSat, Audits, and Support Quality Score all in one view. Red figures flag metrics that need attention.

💡 The Targets panel on the right shows your minimum thresholds at a glance — no need to ask your TL what you're being measured against.
🔍 Weekly Development — Detail View

Click any week row to expand the full breakdown. Every metric for that specific week in one place.

🎯 Targets & Definitions

Always visible alongside your weekly data. Targets show your minimum thresholds. Definitions explain every metric in plain language.

💡 Not sure what a metric means? Scroll the Definitions panel — it's built right into the screen.
📅 My Monthly Development

Your performance rolled up month by month. Click any month row to expand the full detail on the right.

😊 Client Satisfaction

Your CSAT broken down by month and by individual case. See exactly which cases passed (4-5) and which failed (0-3).

💡 Use this view to understand the story behind your score — not just the number itself.
⭐ Support Quality Score Split

Your QA audits listed by date. Click any audit row to see the full breakdown: Case Handling, Compliance, Diagnosis Resolution, Escalation Transfer, and Communication.

👥 Team Performance

Your full team's weekly results in one table. CSAT%, SQS, AHT, IRT Chat, Assignables, CRT%, and Cases Closed — all weeks, all in one view.

📋 Feedback Tracker

Weekly feedback from your Team Leader, recorded and visible here. Shows Operational Feedback, Quality Feedback, and when it was registered.

💡 This is read-only — your TL creates the feedback on their side and it appears here automatically.
✅ My Registered Actions

Action plans your TL has created for your development. Shows the task, start date, due date, status, and any comments.

💡 If this screen shows "No items" it simply means your TL has not created any action plans for you yet.
👤 My Profile

Your personal details — Site, Workday ID, SRT ID, Role, Market, Team Leader, and Team Manager.

💬 Share Your Ideas / Report an Issue

Have a suggestion or spotted a problem? Select the relevant feature area, write your comment, and submit.

🖥️ App Navigation Check

Now that you've seen every screen — let's make sure you know where to find what you need.

1. Where do you find weekly feedback your TL has recorded for you?
My Weekly Development
Feedback Tracker
My Registered Actions
2. Your Registered Actions screen shows "No items". What does this mean?
There is a technical issue with the app
You have completed all your action plans
Your TL has not created any action plans for you yet
3. Which section shows your CSAT broken down case by case?
My Weekly Development
Support Quality Score Split
Client Satisfaction
4. What should you do before a 1:1 with your TL?
Wait for your TL to share your scores during the meeting
Review your weekly development, Feedback Tracker, and Registered Actions in advance
Check only your CSAT score before the meeting
5. Where do you go to share a suggestion about the app?
Contact your Team Leader directly
Send a message in Google Chat
Use "Share your ideas on the app" from the home screen
3 of 7
Section 04

How to Get Started ⏱ 2 min

Follow these steps to access the app and set yourself up for success from day one.

1

Access the App

Open the link provided by your team. The app is accessible via browser — no download required. Bookmark it for easy daily access.

2

Log In With Your Work Credentials

Use your existing GSO/Telus Digital credentials to log in. Your profile will already be set up — data is pre-populated from existing systems.

3

Review Your Dashboard

Your homepage shows your key metrics at a glance: weekly development, CSAT, and Quality Score. Start here every shift.

4

Check Your Feedback Tracker

Navigate to Feedback Tracker regularly to read any weekly feedback your TL has recorded. It's timestamped, permanent, and you may receive it by email too.

5

Review Your Registered Actions

Check My Registered Actions to see any development tasks your TL has created for you — including due dates and status. Use this before every 1:1.

6

Use It Before Your 1:1 Check-In

Before any meeting with your Team Leader, review your weekly development, Feedback Tracker, and Registered Actions. Come prepared with specific data.

7

Submit Ideas or Report Issues

Found something that could be better? Use the "Share your ideas" or "Report an issue" buttons inside the app to give your feedback directly to the platform team.

4 of 7
Section 05

Understanding Your Metrics ⏱ 4 min

Here's what each metric means and how it connects to your performance picture.

MetricWhat It MeasuresWhere It Comes FromView in App
CSATCustomer satisfaction with their support experiencePost-interaction surveys submitted by customersClient Satisfaction
Support Quality ScoreQuality of your case handling evaluated against set criteriaQA reviews of your casesSupport Quality Score
AHTAverage Handle Time — average length of your chatsAutomatically calculated from case dataMy Weekly Development
Weekly DevelopmentYour performance trend across the current weekAutomatically synced from live dataMy Weekly Development
Monthly DevelopmentYour performance trend across the full calendar monthAutomatically synced from live dataMonthly Development
DSatNumber of dissatisfied customer responses in a given periodCSAT survey resultsClient Satisfaction
Operational FeedbackWeekly operational feedback recorded by your TLTL enters this in the GSO Feedback AppFeedback Tracker
Quality FeedbackWeekly quality-focused feedback recorded by your TLTL enters this in the GSO Feedback AppFeedback Tracker
Registered ActionsDevelopment tasks and action plans agreed with your TLTL creates these in the GSO Feedback AppMy Registered Actions
Team PerformanceYour team's collective results to give context to your scoresAggregated team dataTeam Performance
⚠️

Note: If you notice a metric that looks incorrect or out of date, use the Report an Issue button inside the app. Updates may be on a scheduled refresh cycle.

🧠 Quick Knowledge Check

Let's make sure the key metrics are locked in before you move on.

1. What does CSAT measure?
Customer satisfaction with their support experience
The average length of your chats
Your quality score from QA reviews
2. Where does your Support Quality Score come from?
It's calculated automatically from your chat data
QA reviews of your cases
Customer surveys after each interaction
3. What appears in My Registered Actions?
Your weekly CSAT score breakdown
Feedback from customers on your cases
Development tasks and action plans created by your TL
5 of 7
Section 06

Frequently Asked Questions ⏱ 3 min

Answers to the questions agents are most likely to have when getting started.

No setup is required on your end. Your profile and performance data are pre-populated automatically. Simply log in with your existing work credentials and everything will be ready for you.
Performance data is updated automatically on a regular cycle — you don't need to manually refresh anything. If data seems significantly behind, please use the "Report an Issue" feature to flag it.
The Feedback Tracker shows weekly feedback your Team Leader records about your performance — covering both operational and quality areas. Your TL creates this in their GSO Feedback App and it appears in your tracker automatically. It is read-only for you. Your TL also has the option to send it to you by email.
Registered Actions are development tasks or action plans your TL creates for you. They show the task description, start date, due date, current status, and any comments. This section is read-only for agents — your TL manages the status updates on their side.
No — it simply means your TL has not created any action plans for you yet. Once they do, they will appear here automatically. There is nothing you need to do.
Your TL uses the GSO Feedback App separately. The Reviewers App is your personal space — it gives you the same data visibility your TL has about your performance.
Raise this with your Team Leader during your next check-in or via normal escalation channels. The app displays data from existing performance systems — disputes follow the same process as before.
First, try clearing your browser cache and retrying. If the issue persists, contact your Team Leader or use the "Report an Issue" feature. (Detailed support contact — coming soon.)
No. The GSO Reviewers App is a visibility and feedback tool — it does not replace formal performance reviews or HR processes. It gives you better access to your day-to-day data for more informed conversations with your TL.
6 of 7

The GSO Reviewers App is growing. Here are two new features coming soon — built specifically to give you even more visibility and recognition on the floor.

🏆 Agent LeaderboardSee who the top performers are across the entire site. The leaderboard will give you a real-time view of where you rank among your peers — a chance to celebrate strong performance and set a target to aim for.
Coming soon
🔍 Personalized Case Scrubbing OverviewAll feedback your SMEs provide during case scrubbing will be available directly in your app — case by case, in one place. Full transparency on every note.
